methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $3.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.5B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
ElevenLabs is a Series C-stage AI & Software company seeking valuation following a $200M+ fundraise at a $3.0B+ post-money. The relevant comp universe includes six same-sector, same-stage private companies (Figure AI, Skild AI, Neuralink, Luma AI, Runway, and one unnamed peer) and one public comparable (PLTR).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis assumes a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier from the last post-money valuation of $3.0B, implying $5.1B. Peer-median analysis across the six private comparables yields a median valuation of $6.8B, with a range spanning $1.5B to $39.0B. The wide dispersion reflects material differentiation in market positioning and investor conviction within the frontier AI cohort.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $4.3B (low) to $7.9B (high), with a median of $6.0B. This range is supported by both forward-looking stage dynamics and contemporaneous peer benchmarking. Key uncertainties include the appropriate peer weighting—Figure AI trades at a substantial premium at $39.0B while Runway anchors the lower bound at $1.5B—and whether frontier AI generalists merit median multiples or outlier positioning. Revenue trajectory, unit economics, and market share within voice synthesis applications remain critical but unquantified determinants of final pricing.
